data.title: Ethena Agrees With Regulator to Withdraw From German Market
data.description:
• Ethena announced that it has agreed to wind down its operations in Germany, onboarding existing users to its British Virgin Islands entity in the process.
  • Three weeks ago BaFin identified "serious deficiencies" in Ethena's USDe token.
  • ENA is down by 2.88% in the past 24 hours.

data.title: CleanSpark Ditches Bitcoin  'HODL' Strategy to Stop Dilution Via Equity Raise
data.description:
• Bitcoin miner CleanSpark is moving away from its strategy of HODLing 100% of the BTC it mines.
  • "We believe a more effective way to increase shareholder value is through a balanced approach between monetizing new production and building long-term holdings," CEO Zach Bradford said.
  • The company has also increased its credit facility with Coinbase Prime (COIN) to $200 million.

data.title: AI Crypto Tokens Nurse Losses as Nvidia Bearish Options Bets Cross the Tape
data.description:
• Tokens linked to artificial intelligence underperformed major cryptocurrencies in the last 24 hours, with Bittensor's TAO and Render Network's RNDR both declining.
  • Unusual activity in Nvidia's short-duration put options suggests traders are hedging against potential market downturns, Convex Value said.
  • Nvidia announced plans to manufacture AI supercomputers in the U.S., marking a significant shift in AI infrastructure production.

data.title: Zero Hash Processed $2B in Flows to Tokenized Funds as RWA Demand Accelerates
data.description:
• Zero Hash handled $2 billion in tokenized fund flows in four months amid rising institutional demand for on-chain assets.
  • The firm's platform enables stablecoin transactions for tokenized RWA funds from BlackRock, Franklin Templeton and Republic.
  • Tokenized RWA market grew to $20.6B in Q1 2025, with Zero Hash claiming to facilitate 35% of the new flows.

data.title: XRP Most Likely to Get U.S. Spot ETF Approval Ahead of SOL, DOGE: Analysts
data.description:
• XRP and Solana are leading candidates for U.S. spot ETF approval due to their high liquidity, with XRP potentially launching earlier.
  • XRP's U.S. market share has increased since the SEC's 2021 lawsuit, while Solana's share has declined.
  • The recent launch of a 2x XRP ETF by Teucrium has boosted XRP's momentum, despite caution in the options market.

data.title: Bitcoin Mining Profitability Down 7.4% in March as Prices, Transaction Fees Fell: Jefferies
data.description:
• Bitcoin mining profitability fell 7.4% in March, according to Jefferies
  • The drop was due to a 11.2% decline in the average bitcoin price and a 9.1% slump in transaction fees, the report said.
  • Bitcoin thus far in April has outperformed the broader U.S. stock market, possibly due to the declining dollar, said the bank.

data.title: Nomura's Laser Digital Denies Involvement in Mantra Crash
data.description:
• Mantra's OM token lost 90% of its value shortly after $227 million of the token was sent to exchanges.
  • Despite early reports, Nomura Group's Laser Capital has denied sending any OM to exchanges.
  • Mantra's team claim the crash was a result of wider market pressures and a liquidation cascade.

data.title: Bitcoin Eyes Gains Versus Gold After Trendline Breakout, Monero Sees Golden Cross
data.description:
• Gold has outperformed bitcoin over the past 12 weeks, but technical charts suggest this trend may reverse.
  • BTC may catch up with the gold rally in the coming days.
  • Monero has shown a long-term bullish shift with a golden crossover, breaking out of a consolidation pattern.

data.title: Tether, Galaxy, Ledn Dominate CeFi Crypto Lending as DeFi Borrowing Soars, Research Shows
data.description:
• The crypto lending market stood at $36.5 billion in Q4 2024, still down 43% from its 2021 bull market peak, Galaxy reported.
  • DeFi borrowing grew 959% from the 2022 bear market bottom, reaching $19.1 billion across 20 protocols and 12 blockchains.
  • Tether, Galaxy, and Ledn hold nearly 90% of the $11.2 billion outstanding loans by centralized lenders.

data.title: Bitcoin ETFs Lose Over $800M in April as Institutions Stick With Bonds Amid Tariff Volatility
data.description:
• Despite social media calls to buy bitcoin, institutions are not following this trend, as evidenced by significant outflows from U.S.-listed spot BTC ETFs.
  • U.S. Treasury bills continue to attract strong institutional demand, highlighting their status as a safe haven amid economic uncertainty.
  • Rising U.S. recession odds and trade tensions have increased market volatility, affecting corporate earnings guidance and risk asset performance.
